qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL asset mutation sends attacker-chosen requests before the IP-based SSRF guard rejects the upload
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the GraphQL asset remote-upload path in `craft\gql\resolvers\mutations\Asset`, while the advisory targets `UsersController::actionSendActivationEmail()` accepting arbitrary pending-user `userId` values without an ownership or permission check.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's unauthenticated activation-email resend flaw in `UsersController::actionSendActivationEmail()`; the only qualifying finding is an authenticated GraphQL asset-upload SSRF in a different component and code path.
evidence: The advisory and patch identify `UsersController::actionSendActivationEmail()` as the vulnerable endpoint, with the fix removing `send-activation-email` from `allowAnonymous` and routing the action through `userActionChecks()` so it requires a CP POST plus `editUsers` permission.

The only final qualifying finding in `.judge/input_manifest.json` is `graphql_asset_mutation_blind_ssrf`, which traces `POST /actions/graphql/api` into `craft\gql\resolvers\mutations\Asset::handleUpload()` and a premature outbound fetch of attacker-controlled `_file.url` input.
No qualifying finding references `UsersController`, pending-user activation-email flows, arbitrary `userId` resend behavior, or the advisory's missing ownership/authorization check.
